steps.title: Identify the key reversal
steps.content: The passage uses however to challenge an apparent distinction: both private and public libraries, despite seeming different, shared a core similarity—they were patronage extensions.

steps.title: Match the claim to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D — To assert that private and early public libraries in ancient Rome had an essential similarity directly states the passage's purpose: to assert an essential similarity between private and early public libraries.

The other choices either focus on a peripheral detail, add a critical judgment the passage doesn't make, or claim the passage addresses the wrong time period.

That matches Choice D — To assert that private and early public libraries in ancient Rome had an essential similarity.
